name: Homeostatic Reinforcement Learning (Drive-Reduction MDP)
description: A reinforcement learning agent where reward is defined as reduction in a quadratic homeostatic drive function D(x) = phi*(x-s)^2. The agent learns Q-values via temporal-difference (TD) updates in a discretized state space (binned energy × relative food direction) and selects actions via softmax policy. This directly implements Keramati & Gutkin (2011) drive-reduction theory where behavior is instrumentally reinforced by restoring internal state toward the set point. Implements postulates P1 (set points), P2 (error-driven correction), and P6 (reward as drive reduction).

variables.name: energy
variables.description: Current internal energy level of the agent

variables.name: previous_energy
variables.description: Energy level before the last action (for reward computation)

variables.name: reward
variables.description: Drive reduction achieved by last action: D(x_prev) - D(x_curr)

variables.name: q_table
variables.description: Tabular action-value function indexed by discretized state z and action a
range: empty dict; entries initialized to 0.0 on first visit

variables.name: discrete_state
variables.description: Tuple of (energy_bin, food_direction) encoding the discretized state

variables.name: previous_state
variables.description: Discretized state before the last action (for TD update)

parameters.name: drive_weight
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2011): single resource, unit weight

parameters.name: energy_per_eat
parameters.source: Tuned to interaction with decay rate

rules.description: Quadratic drive function: total discomfort as squared deviation from set point
Energy dynamics: passive decay plus food intake
rules.pseudocode: x = clamp(x - d + Delta_eat * (1 if ate else 0), 0, x_max)

rules.description: Drive-reduction reward: positive reward when drive decreases (moving toward set point)
State discretization: bin energy and compute relative food direction
rules.pseudocode:
energy_bin = min(int(x * n_bins / x_max), n_bins - 1)
nearest_food = food item minimizing Manhattan distance to (pos_x, pos_y)
if nearest_food is None: food_dir = 'none'
else if nearest_food at same position: food_dir = 'here'
else: food_dir = compass_direction(pos to nearest_food)  # one of 8 compass strings
z = (energy_bin, food_dir)
delta = r + gamma * max(Q[z_new][a'] for a' in valid_actions) - Q[z_prev][a_prev]

for each action a in actions:
    if a == 'eat' and no food at current position: q_val = -1e9
    else: q_val = Q[z][a]  # default 0.0 if unseen
    logit[a] = beta * q_val
pi[a] = exp(logit[a]) / sum(exp(logit[a']) for a' in actions)
selected_action = sample from pi

decision_logic.description: TD Q-learning agent with drive-reduction reward. On each step: (1) update internal energy, (2) compute drive-reduction reward from the transition, (3) perform TD update on Q-table, (4) discretize current state, (5) select action via softmax over Q-values. The eat action is masked when no food is at the current position. Over time, the agent learns to move toward food when energy is low and rest when near the set point.
pseudocode: ate = (action was 'eat') and (environment reward > 0 or last_action_result indicates success)
x = clamp(x - d + Delta_eat * (1 if ate else 0), 0, x_max)
elif nearest_food.x == position[0] and nearest_food.y == position[1]: food_dir = 'here'
# TD Update (skip on first step when z_prev is None)
max_q_next = max(Q.get((z_new, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions)
delta = r + gamma * max_q_next - Q.get((z_prev, a_prev), 0.0)
Q[(z_prev, a_prev)] = Q.get((z_prev, a_prev), 0.0) + alpha * delta
food_at_pos = any f in food_list where f.x == position[0] and f.y == position[1]
exp_logits = {a: exp(logits[a] - max_logit) for a in actions}
probs = {a: exp_logits[a] / total for a in actions}

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception.resources.food  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_at_position: any f in perception.resources.food where f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y
perception_to_variables.ate_food: if previous action was 'eat': check perception.last_action_result for success or check env reward > 0

env_mapping.reward_source: Internal: drive-reduction r = D(x_prev) - D(x_curr). The environment's ConsumeEffect reward (1.0) is used only as a signal that eating was successful; the agent's decision-making is driven by the internal drive-reduction reward, not the environment reward.

expected_behaviors.description: Drive increases as energy decays without eating, generating negative reward for inaction
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 20 steps with no food → assert D increases each step, r is negative each step

expected_behaviors.description: Eating when below set point produces large positive drive-reduction reward
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set x=50, eat food → compute D_before and D_after → assert r > 0 and r is large

expected_behaviors.description: Over many episodes, Q-values for moving toward food when hungry become higher than Q-values for staying
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 500 steps → for low energy bins with food_dir != 'none', assert Q[(low_bin, dir), move_toward] > Q[(low_bin, dir), 'stay']

expected_behaviors.description: Agent learns to stay when energy is near set point (satiation)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 500 steps → for energy bins near s, assert action probabilities favor 'stay' over movement

expected_behaviors.description: Eating when above set point is not reinforced (drive increases if x > s and eating pushes further away)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set x=90 (above s=80), eat food → D_after > D_before → r < 0 → agent learns to avoid eating when satiated

expected_behaviors.description: Early exploration transitions to exploitation as Q-values converge
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: measure action entropy at step 10 vs step 500 → entropy should decrease over time

references: Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2011) — A Reinforcement Learning Theory for Homeostatic Regulation — NeurIPS Proceedings
Yin, H. (2025) — Linking Homeostasis to Reinforcement Learning: Internal State Control and Behavior — ScienceDirect
Sutton, R. & Barto, A. — Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction (TD learning, Q-learning conventions)
Gross, F. et al. (2024) — A Functional Approach to Homeostatic Regulation — Biology Direct
Cannon, W.B. (1932) — The Wisdom of the Body
